topics.keywords_en: tax extension 2026,file tax extension online,form 4868 2026,irs tax extension deadline,automatic tax extension
topics.intro_en: Missing the April tax deadline without an extension means a 5% per month failure-to-file penalty. Filing an extension takes 5 minutes online and eliminates that penalty entirely.

topics.h2a_en: How to File a Tax Extension Online
topics.body1_en: TaxExtension.com handles the Form 4868 filing electronically. You need your name, address, Social Security number, and an estimate of your tax liability. The IRS accepts it immediately and you're covered through October 15.

topics.verdict_title_en: TaxExtension.com — Fastest Way to File Your Extension
topics.verdict_desc_en: Five minutes, no explanation needed, instantly accepted by the IRS. Filing an extension is always the right move when you're not ready by April 15.
topics.h2b_en: What Happens If You Miss the Extension Deadline
topics.body2_en: If you miss April 15 without filing an extension, the failure-to-file penalty starts immediately at 5% per month on unpaid taxes. This compounds up to 25% maximum. Filing the extension — even if you can't pay — eliminates this penalty entirely.

faqs_en.q: How long does a tax extension last?
faqs_en.a: A federal tax extension gives you 6 additional months — from April 15 to October 15.

faqs_en.q: Do I need to pay taxes when I file an extension?
faqs_en.a: You should pay an estimate of what you owe. The extension covers the filing deadline but not the payment deadline.

faqs_en.q: Is filing a tax extension bad?
faqs_en.a: No. Extensions are routine. The IRS processes millions each year with no negative consequences for the filer.
